subject: Sociology
title: Stereotypes in Media
description: A 60-second lesson on Stereotypes in Media.

hook.text: The dumb blonde. The angry Black man. The nerdy Asian. Media created these shortcuts — and society believed them.

buildup.text: Stereotypes are oversimplified images of social groups repeated so often they begin to seem natural and true.

discovery.text: Media stereotypes shape hiring, policing, and daily life. People judge real individuals through fictional templates.

twist.text: Even positive stereotypes harm. Model minority myths erase struggle and pit minority groups against each other.

climax.text: Stereotypes in media aren't harmless entertainment. They are blueprints for prejudice that shape how society functions.

punchline.text: Fiction becomes prejudice when repeated enough.

quiz.question: Why are even 'positive' stereotypes harmful?
options: They erase individual experience and can pit groups against each other
They always help the stereotyped group
